# CloudFront Management Terraform Module




This module manages AWS CloudFront distributions, including origins, behaviors, functions, and staging configurations.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration. 
[<img align="right" title="Share via Email" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/ios-mail.svg"/>][share_email]

[![Terraform Open Source Modules](https://docs.cloudops.works/images/terraform-open-source-modules.svg)][terraform_modules]



It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

A comprehensive module to manage AWS CloudFront distributions with support for multiple origins (S3 and custom), cache behaviors, CloudFront functions, and continuous deployment through staging distributions. It simplifies the setup of complex CloudFront configurations by providing a structured way to define settings and functions.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-aws-cloudfront-management/releases).


### Terragrunt Configuration

To use this module with Terragrunt, create a `terragrunt.hcl` file with the following structure:

```hcl
include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-aws-cloudfront-management?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  # (Optional) The name of the CloudFront distribution.
  name = "my-cdn"

  # (Optional) Prefix for the distribution name.
  name_prefix = "cdn"

  # (Required) Organization details for tagging and naming.
  org = {
    organization_name = "my-org"
    organization_unit = "my-unit"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "prod"
  }

  # (Optional) Is this a hub or spoke configuration? Default: false
  is_hub = false

  # (Optional) Spoke ID Number. Default: "001"
  spoke_def = "001"

  # (Optional) Extra tags to add to the resources. Default: {}
  extra_tags = {
    Project = "Phoenix"
  }

  # (Optional) ACM certificate ARN for the distribution.
  acm_certificate_arn = "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/..."

  # (Required) CloudFront distribution settings.
  settings = {
    default_origin = "main-origin"
    origins = {
      main-origin = {
        type = "s3"
        name = "my-web-content-bucket"
      }
    }
    default_cache_behavior = {
      managed_cache = "Managed-CachingOptimized"
    }
  }
}
```

### Variables Structure Documentation (YAML)

```yaml
# Full configuration structure for 'settings' variable
settings:
  enabled: true             # (Optional) Enable/disable distribution. Default: true
  ipv6_enabled: false       # (Optional) Enable/disable IPv6. Default: false
  comment: "My CDN"         # (Optional) Description of the distribution.
  default_root_object: "index.html" # (Optional) Default root object. Default: "index.html"
  aliases: ["cdn.example.com"] # (Optional) CNAME aliases. Default: []
  default_origin: "origin1" # (Required) ID of the default origin.
  origins:                  # (Required) Map of origins.
    origin1:
      type: "s3"            # (Required) s3 or custom.
      name: "my-bucket"     # (Required if type is s3) S3 bucket name.
      domain_name: "example.com" # (Required if type is custom) Domain name.
      origin_path: "/web"   # (Optional) Path in the origin.
      origin_staging_path: "/stg" # (Optional) Path for staging origin.
      signing_behavior: "always" # (Optional) always, never, no-override.
      signing_protocol: "sigv4"  # (Optional) sigv4.
  cert:                     # (Optional) Certificate settings.
    cloudfront_default_certificate: true
    minimum_protocol_version: "TLSv1.2_2025"
    ssl_support_method: "sni-only"
  default_cache_behavior:   # (Required) Default cache behavior.
    managed_cache: "Managed-CachingOptimized"
    viewer_protocol_policy: "redirect-to-https"
    allowed_methods: ["GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "PUT", "POST", "PATCH", "DELETE"]
    cached_methods: ["GET", "HEAD"]
    compress: true
    functions:              # (Optional) Function associations.
      - ref: "my-function"  # Reference to a function in 'functions' variable
        event_type: "viewer-request"
  staging:                  # (Optional) Staging distribution settings.
    create: false
    enabled: true
    header:
      name: "X-Staging"
      value: "true"

# Full configuration structure for 'functions' variable
functions:
  my-function:
    comment: "My JS Function"
    runtime: "cloudfront-js-2.0"
    publish: true
    code: |
      function handler(event) {
        var request = event.request;
        return request;
      }
```

## Quick Start

1. Define your S3 bucket or custom origin.
2. Configure your Terragrunt inputs focusing on `org` and `settings`.
3. Ensure `default_origin` matches a key in the `origins` map.
4. Run `terragrunt plan` and then `terragrunt apply`.


## Examples

### Complete Terragrunt Example with Custom Origin and Functions

```hcl
terraform {
  source = "github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-aws-cloudfront-management?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  name_prefix = "myapp"
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_type  = "prod"
    environment_name  = "global"
  }
  settings = {
    default_origin = "app-api"
    origins = {
      app-api = {
        type = "custom"
        domain_name = "api.acme.com"
      }
    }
    default_cache_behavior = {
      managed_cache   = "Managed-CachingOptimized"
      allowed_methods = ["GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS"]
      viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https"
      functions = [
        {
          ref = "security-headers"
          event_type = "viewer-response"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
  functions = {
    security-headers = {
      code = <<EOF
function handler(event) {
    var response = event.response;
    var headers = response.headers;
    headers['x-frame-options'] = { value: 'DENY' };
    headers['x-content-type-options'] = { value: 'nosniff' };
    return response;
}
EOF
    }
  }
}
```



## Makefile Targets
```
Available targets:

  help                                Help screen
  help/all                            Display help for all targets
  help/short                          This help short screen
  init/aws                            Initialize the project for a specific cloud provider: AWS
  init/azurerm                        Initialize the project for a specific cloud provider: Azure RM
  init/gcp                            Initialize the project for a specific cloud provider: GCP
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_acm_certificate_arn"></a> [acm\_certificate\_arn](#input\_acm\_certificate\_arn) | n/a | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_default_ssl"></a> [default\_ssl](#input\_default\_ssl) | n/a | <pre>object({<br/>    enabled           = bool<br/>    cn                = string<br/>    san               = optional(list(string), [])<br/>    auto_validation   = optional(bool, false)<br/>    validation_method = optional(string, "DNS")<br/>    validation_domain = optional(string, "")<br/>    validation_email  = optional(string, "")<br/>  })</pre> | <pre>{<br/>  "auto_validation": false,<br/>  "cn": "",<br/>  "enabled": false,<br/>  "san": [],<br/>  "validation_domain": "example.com",<br/>  "validation_email": "",<br/>  "validation_method": "DNS"<br/>}</pre> | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_functions"></a> [functions](#input\_functions) | A map of CloudFront functions to be associated with the distribution. Each key in the map should be a unique identifier for the function, and the value should contain the configuration for that function. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_name"></a> [name](#input\_name) | The name of the CloudFront distribution. If not provided, a name will be generated using the name\_prefix and system\_name. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_name_prefix"></a> [name\_prefix](#input\_name\_prefix) | The prefix for the CloudFront distribution name. This is used to generate a name if the name variable is not provided. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | CloudFront distribution settings for the project. This variable should contain all the necessary configuration for the CloudFront distribution, such as origins, behaviors, and other settings. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_cloudfront_distribution_arn"></a> [cloudfront\_distribution\_arn](#output\_cloudfront\_distribution\_arn) | The ARN of the CloudFront distribution. |
| <a name="output_cloudfront_distribution_domain_name"></a> [cloudfront\_distribution\_domain\_name](#output\_cloudfront\_distribution\_domain\_name) | The domain name of the CloudFront distribution. |
| <a name="output_cloudfront_distribution_id"></a> [cloudfront\_distribution\_id](#output\_cloudfront\_distribution\_id) | The ID of the CloudFront distribution. |
| <a name="output_cloudfront_distribution_status"></a> [cloudfront\_distribution\_status](#output\_cloudfront\_distribution\_status) | The status of the CloudFront distribution. |
| <a name="output_cloudfront_etag"></a> [cloudfront\_etag](#output\_cloudfront\_etag) | The ETag of the CloudFront distribution. This is a unique identifier for the distribution's configuration and can be used for caching or versioning purposes. |
| <a name="output_cloudfront_hosted_zone_id"></a> [cloudfront\_hosted\_zone\_id](#output\_cloudfront\_hosted\_zone\_id) | The Route 53 Hosted Zone ID for the CloudFront distribution. |
